title: Akamai Joins Growing Chorus of Vendors Betting Big on Secure Enterprise Browsers
summary: When Akamai announced its LayerX acquisition, the company joined a growing list of vendors adding secure enterprise browsers to their product portfolios.

title: Verizon DBIR: Healthcare Fends Off Increased Social Engineering Attacks
summary: Ransomware and vendor breaches persist. The "2026 Data Breach Investigations Report" (DBIR) highlights how evolving social engineering tactics make the sector more vulnerable.

title: China's Webworm Uses Discord, Microsoft Graphs to Hack EU Governments
summary: The advanced persistent threat group also relied on SOCKS proxies like SoftEther VPN, tunneling tools that act as a middleman between victim and attacker.

title: Google API Keys Remain Active After Deletion
summary: A security researcher discovered the API keys can still be used for up to 23 minutes after deletion, even though the cloud provider claims deletion is immediate.

title: AI Agents Are Shifting Identity Security Budget Dynamics
summary: AI agent projects are proliferating throughout the enterprise, and those AI agent identities require management, security, and governance. New Omdia research shows the AI agent identity budget dynamics are very different than traditional IAM projects.

title: Chinese APTs Share Linux Backdoor in Central Asia Telco Attacks
summary: "Showboat" doesn't show off, but clearly it doesn't need to, as it's long helped China spy on small market communications providers.

title: Content Delivery Exploit Opens Websites to Brand Hijacking
summary: The Underminr domain-fronting attack allows threat actors to modify Web requests and leverage trusted websites to cloak malicious activity.

title: Cyber Pros Can't Decide If AI Is a Good or a Bad Thing
summary: There is nothing cybersecurity professionals are more excited about, and nothing they fear more, than AI.

title: GitHub Confirms Breach, 4K Internal Repos Stolen
summary: GitHub confirmed a data breach this week involving the theft of thousands of developer code repositories. One threat actor — TeamPCP — took credit.

title: Fake Android Apps Commit Carrier Billing Fraud for Premium Services
summary: The disguised apps use WebView automation, JavaScript injection, and OTP interception to avoid detection and complete fraudulent subscriptions.

title: Processes & Culture Top Reasons Behind Data Breaches
summary: In spite of state laws meant to improve cyber hygiene, an analysis of incidents shows issues persist and visibility falls short.

title: Patch Now: Critical Flaw in OT Robot OS Gives Attackers Control
summary: An unauthenticated attacker can exploit the command injection vulnerability to gain remote access to robotic systems, causing significant disruption to the environment.

title: Infosecurity Europe
How CISOs Should Prep for Agentic-Ready AI BOMs
summary: Finding ways to document both component and execution attributes for AI bills of materials (AI BOMs).

title: Interpol's 'Operation Ramz' Pioneers Cross-Region Collabs in Middle East
summary: While the numbers are modest, the crackdown on cybercrime involved 13 countries in the MENA region, the largest law enforcement collaboration to date.

title: What It'll Take to Make AI BOMs Usable in a Modern Security Program
summary: Five ways CISOs can prepare for consuming AI bills of materials and influence the direction of how they're generated.

title: What Will Make AI BOMs Real?
summary: A brief overview of the forces at play that will get more organizations on board with creating and consuming AI bill of materials (BOM).

title: Verizon DBIR: Enterprises Face a Dangerous Vulnerability Glut
summary: Verizon's 2026 Data Breach Investigations Report (DBIR) finds that exploits are now involved in 31% of initial access for breaches, while patching lags too far behind the bad guys.

title: Windows Zero-Day Barrage Continues After Patch Tuesday
summary: YellowKey, GreenPlasma, and MiniPlasma add to the growing list of vulnerabilities a security researcher disclosed over the past six weeks.

title: CISA Exposes Secrets, Credentials in 'Private' Repo
summary: The agency's GitHub repository, publicly available since November 2025, was ironically named "Private-CISA."

title: Stealer Spoofs Google, Microsoft & Apple, Then Backdoors macOS
summary: The SHub Reaper stealer, which hides behind fake WeChat and Miro installers, marks a shift from ClickFix social engineering to Apple script-based execution.

title: Looking Back, Looking Forward: Digesting a Dynamic Bouillabaisse of Cyber Evolution
summary: Dark Reading editors reflect on two decades of dramatic change — from perimeter defense to assume-breach strategies — and warn that while AI, cloud, and COVID-19 have transformed the threat landscape, organizations are still failing at fundamental security hygiene that could stop sophisticated attacks in their tracks.

title: Is 2026 the Year AI Bills of Materials Get Real?
summary: Understanding AI BOMs and where they fit into risk management for artificial intelligence.

title: Microsoft Exchange Zero-Day Under Attack, No Patch Available
summary: CVE-2026-42897 stems from a cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability and can allow an attacker to compromise Outlook Web Access (OWA) mailboxes.

title: 'Claw Chain' Vulnerabilities Threaten OpenClaw Deployments
summary: The now-patched vulnerabilities in the rapidly growing AI agent framework allow attackers to steal credentials, escalate privileges, and maintain persistence.

title: Shai-Hulud Worm Clones Spread After Code Release
summary: The release of Shai-Hulud source code spells trouble for software developers as researchers worry the self-replicating worm could scale.

title: Fuel Tank Breaches Expand Scope of Iran's Cyber Offensive
summary: Security experts have long warned that insecure automatic tank gauge (ATG) systems exposed on the Internet can be tampered with by threat actors.

title: The Boring Stuff Is Dangerous Now
summary: AI agents capable of discovering and exploiting obscure vulnerabilities are emerging alongside developers producing vast amounts of potentially flawed AI-generated code, forcing defenders to adapt accordingly.

title: Boulevard of Broken Dreams: 2 Decades of Cyber Fails
summary: From the MGM and Caesars fiasco and MOVEit's patch nightmare to epic business blunders and the jaded reality of living in a post-breach world, Dark Reading looks back at the mistakes, miscalculations, systemic failures, and cringeworthy moments that still have us shaking our heads.

title: Can Laws Stop Deepfakes? South Korea Aims to Find Out
summary: South Korea's local elections next month will be a test bed for how effective regulations might be to stymie the flow of deepfakes.

title: Congress Puts Heat on Instructure After Canvas Outage
summary: The House Committee on Homeland Security sent a letter about the Canvas cyberattack, the same day that the edtech company said it reached an "agreement" with the ShinyHunters cybercriminals.

title: Cyber Pioneers Ponder Past as Prologue
summary: Robert "RSnake" Hansen, Katie Moussouris, Rich Mogull, Richard Stiennon, and Bruce Schneier reflect on how their favorite columns penned for Dark Reading over the past 20 years have stood the test of time.

title: Taiwan Bullet Train Hack Highlights Cybersecurity Gaps in Rail Systems
summary: A Taiwanese student experimenting with software-defined radio technology shut down three bullet trains for nearly an hour, leading to an anti-terrorism response.

title: SecurityScorecard Snags Driftnet to Level Up Threat Intelligence
summary: The acquisition looks to boost visibility into third-party ecosystems, which are becoming a bigger concern as vectors for supply chain attacks.

title: Maximum Severity Cisco SD-WAN Bug Exploited in the Wild
summary: This is the second time this year a threat actor has leveraged a CVSS 10.0 vulnerability in Cisco's network control system.

title: [Virtual Event] Anatomy of a Data Breach: What to Do if it Happens to You
'FrostyNeighbor' APT Carefully Targets Govt Orgs in Poland, Ukraine
summary: Attackers uniquely fingerprint victims before delivering spear-phishing payloads aimed at espionage, in the latest campaign from the Belarussian nation-state threat group.

title: AI Drives Cybersecurity Investments, Widening 'Valley of Death'
summary: In a role reversal, investment dollars in security startups exceeded the value of mergers and acquisitions in 1Q26 by more than $1 billion, a rare occurrence.

title: Foxconn Attack Highlights Manufacturing's Cyber Crisis
summary: A Nitrogen ransomware attack on Foxconn's North American facilities is one of 600 hits on manufacturers this year, as gangs increasingly target the sector for its low tolerance for downtime.

title: Checkbox Assessments Aren't Fit to Measure Risk
summary: Security governance needs to be more than an annual compliance exercise. New companies are emerging to address risk-management gaps in current audit tools.

title: Attackers Weaponize RubyGems for Data Dead Drops
summary: Threat actors are publishing RubyGems packages that include scrapers targeting public-facing UK government servers, but with no clear objective.

title: Tables Turn on 'The Gentlemen' RaaS Gang With Data Leak
summary: An OPSEC failure provides a window into what helped the ransomware group rise: a generous affiliate model, opportunistic TTPs, and an effective organizational structure.

title: Dark Reading Celebrates 20 Years as a Leading Authority on Cybersecurity, Highlighting the People, Events, Ideas, and Technologies Shaping the Modern Risk Landscape
summary: Informa TechTarget's flagship cybersecurity media brand launches a special content series to mark two decades as a trusted source for cybersecurity professionals.

title: LatAm Vibe Hackers Generate Custom Hacking Tools on the Fly
summary: In the latest evolution of automated cyberattacks, threat actors heavily leveraged AI agents to support campaigns against entities in Mexico and Brazil.

title: China's 'FamousSparrow' APT Nests in South Caucasus Energy Firm
summary: The cyberthreat group targets an Azerbaijani oil and gas firm with repeated attacks, as the China-linked actors extend targeting beyond hospitality, telecom, and government sectors.

title: It's Patch Tuesday for Microsoft & Not a Zero-Day In Sight
summary: It's the first time in two years with no zero-days. But with 137 flaws to patch, including nine critical ones, admins still have plenty of work to do.

title: Hugging Face Packages Weaponized With a Single File Tweak
summary: A tokenizer library file present in Hugging Face AI models can be manipulated to hijack the model's outputs and exfiltrate data.

title: 20 Leaders Who Built the CISO Era: 2 Decades of Change
summary: As part of Dark Reading's 20th anniversary special coverage, we profile the CISOs, founders, researchers, criminals, and policymakers who rewrote the enterprise risk playbook.

title: Worm Redux: Fresh Mini Shai-Hulud Infections Bite Supply Chain
summary: Hundreds of npm packages infected by the self-propagating, credential-stealing worm from TeamPCP are related to the open source TanStack ecosystem.

title: FCC Softens Ban on Foreign-Made Routers
summary: The Federal Communications Commission eased some restrictions and pushed back deadlines for foreign router manufacturers, but the ban is still in place.
